comment: Quality Gates catalog -- security dimension. v0.12.0 ships stub rows only (NOT-VERIFIED + waiver until 2026-07-26 tracked_in v0.12.1 SEC-* placeholders). v0.12.1 MIGRATE-03 ships the verifier scripts + flips status to PASS. Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. CLAUDE.md threat-model section drives the contract: outbound HTTP allowlist + audit-log immutability are the two P0 surfaces this dimension defends.

sources: CLAUDE.md threat-model section -- Outbound HTTP allowlist

asserts: v0.12.1 stub -- a fuzz test attempts outbound HTTP to an origin not in REPOSIX_ALLOWED_ORIGINS and the http::client() factory rejects with the documented error
the disallowed_methods clippy lint catches direct reqwest::Client::new outside core/http.rs (already covered by code/clippy-lint-loaded; this row is the runtime allowlist test)

rows.owner_hint: P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): quality/gates/security/allowlist-enforcement.sh now EXISTS (was dangling per R2 § D/H finding #3) -- wraps `cargo test -p reposix-core --test http_allowlist` (13 real #[tokio::test] fns already asserting egress-rejected/redirect-recheck/env-override/loopback-allow, crates/reposix-core/tests/http_allowlist.rs) plus a static grep that clippy.toml bans direct reqwest::Client construction AND that production request_with_headers_and_body() checks load_allowlist_from_env() BEFORE self.inner.request(...) (crates/reposix-core/src/http.rs:305-311). The script has NOT been executed via a real cargo run in this dispatch (no-cargo constraint); waiver stays renewed until a real pre-pr/CI run confirms PASS -- explicit P92 line item.

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): the verifier script landed this commit (was fully dangling before), but its exit code has not been confirmed by an actual `cargo test` run (CLAUDE.md build-memory-budget forbids ad-hoc cargo during this F-class framework dispatch). Manual code inspection confirms the real behavioral test file + the static-grep targets both already exist and look correct. Renewing as a safety net until a real cargo run (tracked as an explicit P92 line item) confirms the wrapper script itself has no integration bug. The 'v0.12.1 SEC-01' label was dead -- repointed per D90-02(c)/(d) mass-renewal rule.
waiver.tracked_in: P92 (Push-flow correctness) -- explicit security-e2e verifier-wrapper confirmation line item, quality/reports/raise-list-p90.md § 5

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the allowlist gate actually failed to reject a non-allowlisted origin, `cargo test -p reposix-core --test http_allowlist` would report a FAILED test (e.g. egress_to_non_allowlisted_host_is_rejected) instead of Error::InvalidOrigin, and the wrapper script's asserts_failed array would name it directly.

sources: CLAUDE.md threat-model section -- Audit log is append-only
rows.command: cargo test -p reposix-core --test audit_schema && cargo test -p reposix-cache --test audit_is_append_only

asserts: v0.12.1 stub -- a test attempts UPDATE/DELETE against either audit table and the SQLite layer rejects (append-only by schema)
both tables present in the test fixture

rows.owner_hint: P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): quality/gates/security/audit-immutability.sh now EXISTS (was dangling per R2 § D/H finding #3) -- wraps `cargo test -p reposix-core --test audit_schema` (8 tests: append-only triggers reject UPDATE/DELETE, H-02 schema-attack hardening) AND `cargo test -p reposix-cache --test audit_is_append_only` (audit_events_cache counterpart), plus a static grep confirming WAL mode on the cache-side connection (crates/reposix-cache/src/db.rs:54-55). NOTE asymmetry: reposix-core::audit::open_audit_db does NOT set WAL explicitly -- only the cache-side connection is WAL-tuned; the script only claims WAL where it's real. Script has NOT been executed via a real cargo run in this dispatch (no-cargo constraint); waiver stays renewed until a real pre-pr/CI run confirms PASS -- explicit P92 line item.

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): the verifier script landed this commit (was fully dangling before), but its exit code has not been confirmed by an actual `cargo test` run (CLAUDE.md build-memory-budget forbids ad-hoc cargo during this F-class framework dispatch). Manual code inspection confirms both real test files (crates/reposix-core/tests/audit_schema.rs, crates/reposix-cache/tests/audit_is_append_only.rs) already exist and look correct against both audit tables. Renewing as a safety net until a real cargo run (tracked as an explicit P92 line item) confirms the wrapper script itself has no integration bug. The 'v0.12.1 SEC-02' label was dead -- repointed per D90-02(c)/(d) mass-renewal rule.
waiver.tracked_in: P92 (Push-flow correctness) -- explicit security-e2e verifier-wrapper confirmation line item, quality/reports/raise-list-p90.md § 5

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If either audit table actually accepted an UPDATE/DELETE, `audit_schema.rs`'s or `audit_is_append_only.rs`'s trigger-rejection assertion would fail to see an 'append-only' error and the row survival count would drop -- a real schema regression is the falsifying evidence, not an inferred one.

rows.comment: Stage-2 catalog-row minting (2026-07-04). Substrate already exists (commit a0c84a3): connector_audit_wired_confluence / connector_audit_wired_jira tests in reposix-remote's backend_dispatch.rs.

asserts: cargo test -p reposix-remote connector_audit_wired exits 0 and runs >=2 tests
a static grep of crates/reposix-remote/src/backend_dispatch.rs confirms build_confluence and build_jira (the non-test production dispatch paths) each call .with_audit(...) before returning

rows.owner_hint: if RED: build_confluence or build_jira in crates/reposix-remote/src/backend_dispatch.rs stopped calling .with_audit(...) before returning the connector, or the connector_audit_wired_* tests regressed -- OP-3 (CLAUDE.md) requires every dispatched write-capable connector to have audit_events wired
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs `cargo test -p reposix-remote connector_audit_wired` (>=2 tests, each building a real Confluence/JIRA connector via the production build_confluence/build_jira functions and asserting backend.has_audit() plus a real audit_events table row) AND greps backend_dispatch.rs to confirm build_confluence/build_jira (outside #[cfg(test)]) call .with_audit(...). If the claim ('production dispatch wires audit for write-capable real connectors') were false -- e.g. someone removed .with_audit(audit) from build_jira -- the cargo test would fail its has_audit() assertion OR the grep would find zero with_audit call sites in the production functions, and the verifier would exit non-zero.
